temporal_description: A close-up shot focuses on a man in a dimly lit room. He is wearing a light blue collared shirt and a dark tie. He holds a black, old-fashioned telephone receiver to his ear with his right hand, his expression serious and intent. In the upper left corner of the screen, white Chinese characters, "风行网," are visible. The background is dark and out of focus, with the horizontal slats of a window blind visible on the right. The camera is steady, creating a tense and focused atmosphere. The audio features a tense and serious exchange between two men. A man speaks with a firm, commanding voice, his tone urgent and authoritative. He gives a direct order, "Use the channel just now to notify Da Xiang one more time, saying the operation is canceled." The scene cuts to a medium shot of a different man standing in what appears to be an office or a classroom. He is dressed in a dark suit, a white shirt, and a patterned tie. He looks slightly to his left with a concerned or surprised expression. The room is sparsely furnished, with a whiteboard covered in diagrams and protected by plastic wrap on the left wall. The lighting is stark, casting deep shadows. The white Chinese characters, "风行网," remain in the upper left corner. The camera is static. The shot returns to the first man, now seen from a slightly wider angle. He is still on the phone, gesturing with his free hand as he speaks. He is standing near a window with closed blinds. The camera remains steady, maintaining the close and intense focus on the man and his actions. Another man responds with a quick, clear, and subservient "Yes, sir," acknowledging the order with immediate compliance. The background is quiet, with no music or other distracting sounds, emphasizing the gravity of the conversation.
